cause: The current SSL implementation does not support the configured SSL minimum protocol version.
action: No action is required.

message: Certicom SSL is available, however JSSE is being used for SSL, since JSSE has been selected by system property {0}, either explicitly or by default.
cause: The system property {0} is selecting JSSE either explicitly or by default.
action: To use Certicom for SSL, set the system property {0} to false.

message: JSSE has not been selected by system property {0}, however Certicom SSL is not available, so JSSE is being used for SSL.
cause: Certicom SSL is not available, so JSSE is being used for SSL, even though JSSE was not selected.
action: To use Certicom for SSL, install Certicom.

message: Certicom SSL is available, however JSSE is being used for SSL, since JSSE has been selected by the SSLMBean.JSSEEnabled attribute, either explicitly or by default.
cause: The attribute SSLMBean.JSSEEnabled is selecting JSSE either explicitly or by default.
action: To use Certicom for SSL, set the attribute SSLMBean.JSSEEnabled to false.

message: JSSE has not been selected by the SSLMBean.JSSEEnabled attribute, however Certicom SSL is not available, so JSSE is being used for SSL.
cause: Certicom SSL is not available, so JSSE is being used for SSL, even though JSSE was not selected.
action: To use Certicom for SSL, install Certicom.

message: JSSE has been selected by default, since the SSLMBean is not available.
cause: This usually occurs early in server startup, before Management Service runtime access is available.
action: No action is required. Eventually the Management Service will start and normal operation will commence.

message: JSSE has been selected by default, since the system property {0} has the invalid value "{1}".
cause: The system property {0} was not set to "true" or "false".
action: Change the system property {0} value to be either "true" or "false".

message: Illegal key store source value [keyStoreType="{0}", source="{1}", exception="{2}", message="{3}"]
cause: The configured key store source value is not valid for the key store type.
action: Correct the key store source value, ensuring that the source is appropriate for the key store type. For example, the "jks" key store type requires that the source be a file path to an existing key store file.

message: Illegal or unknown key store type value [keyStoreType="{0}", source="{1}"]
cause: The configured key store type value is not recognized.
action: Correct the key store type value. For example, the "jks" key store type requires the type value to be "jks".

message: Unable to instantiate key store [keyStoreType="{0}", provider="{1}", exception="{2}", message="{3}"]
cause: Key store instantiation failure usually occurs because no appropriate specified or default provider is available. See the exception for details.
action: Verify that key store type is correct, and that a supporting Java Cryptography Architecture (JCA) provider is available in the Java Runtime Environment. A "jks" key store provider is usually available by default. An Oracle Key Store Service (type "kss") provider is only available if Oracle Java Required Files (JRF) is installed.

message: Unable to load key store [keyStoreType="{0}", source="{1}", exception="{2}", message="{3}"]
cause: A key store could fail to load due to an invalid source or password. Since key store instantiation succeeded, the key store type is valid. See the exception for details.
action: Check the configured key store source and password values.

message: One of the Custom Identity Key Store attributes is not set on the channel override (NetworkAccessPointMBean). If any of the following attributes are set, then all of these attributes must be set: CustomIdentityKeyStoreFileName, CustomIdentityKeyStoreType, and either CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhrase or CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhraseEncrypted.
cause: One of the following Custom Identity Key Store attributes are not set: CustomIdentityKeyStoreFileName, CustomIdentityKeyStoreType, and either CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhrase or CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhraseEncrypted.
action: Ensure all of the following attributes are set: CustomIdentityKeyStoreFileName, CustomIdentityKeyStoreType, and either CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhrase or CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhraseEncrypted.

message: When overriding Custom Identity Key Store attributes, ChannelIdentityCustomized must be enabled.
cause: ChannelIdentityCustomized is not enabled, and must be when the following Custom Identity Key Store attributes are set: CustomIdentityKeyStoreFileName, CustomIdentityKeyStoreType, and either CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhrase or CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhraseEncrypted.
action: Enable ChannelIdentityCustomized.

message: When overriding Custom Identity Key Store attributes, CustomPrivateKeyAlias must be set on the channel override (NetworkAccessPointMBean).
cause: CustomPrivateKeyAlias is not set on the NetworkAccessPointMBean, and must be when the following Custom Identity Key Store attributes are set: CustomIdentityKeyStoreFileName, CustomIdentityKeyStoreType, and either CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhrase or CustomIdentityKeyStorePassPhraseEncrypted.
action: Set the CustomPrivateKeyAlias attribute on the NetworkAccessPointMBean.

message: Unable to obtain private key with alias {2} from {1} keystore file : {0}
cause: Either KeyStoreFileName or KeyStorePassPhrase or KeyStoreAlias or KeyStore private key password is incorrect.
action: Verify that the path to the keystore, the keystore type, the password for the keystore, the alias for the keystore, and the password for the private key were specified correctly.

message: Loading certificates and private key from {1} keystore file {0} caused an Exception :{2}
cause: Either KeyStoreFileName or KeyStorePassPhrase or KeyStoreAlias or KeyStore private key password is incorrect.
action: Verify that the path to the keystore, the keystore type, the password for the keystore, the alias for the keystore, and the password for the private key were specified correctly.

message: Could not load a {1} keystore from the file {0}. Exception: {2}
cause: There is an I/O or format problem with the keystore data.
action: Verify that the path to the keystore, the keystore type, and the password for the keystore were specified correctly. Note that some keystore types (for example, JKS) do not require a password for read-only access. Read the exception text for more help on diagnosing the problem. Reboot the server after correcting the problem.

message: Security restarting security realm: {0}
cause: This message is for informational purposes.
action: Verify that the correct security realm is being restarted.

message: Security completed restarting security realm: {0}
cause: This message is for informational purposes.
action: Verify that the correct security realm is restarted.

message: Authentication failure: The specified user failed to log in.
cause: The specified username, password, or both are incorrect.
action: Verify the username and password.

message: The JWTToken is invalid.
cause: JWT token could not satisfy the rule.
action: See the log for detailed information and take appropriate action.

message: JWTIdentityAsserter received unknown token data, data should by byte[].
cause: An invalid JWT token was supplied.
action: Supply a valid JWT token.

message: The JWT token cannot pass the sign validation.
cause: An invalid JWT token was supplied.
action: Supply a valid JWT token.

message: Partitions require a realm with updated default role mapper and authorizations policies. The realm {0} does not contain the correct default policies. Create a new realm and reapply any custom policies.
cause: Partition support requires updated default role mapping and authorization policies.
action: Create a new realm with the same providers as the existing realm. Transfer the custom security policies from the old realm to the new realm and set the partition realm attribute to point to the new realm.

message: The provider {1} in realm {0} does not support identity domains via the IdentityDomainAwareProviderMBean interface. If identity domains are configured in the domain, then all role mapper, authorization, credential mapper, and audit providers must support this interface.
cause: The configured providers do not support identity domains.
action: Ensure that all configured role mapper, authorization, credential mapper, and audit providers support identity domains. Remove any providers that do not support identity domains.

message: Inconsistent security configuration, the provider {1} in security realm {0} does not support identity domains via the IdentityDomainAwareProviderMBean interface. If identity domains are configured in the domain, then all role mapper, authorization, credential mapper, and audit providers must support this interface.
cause: The configured providers do not support identity domains.
action: Ensure that all configured role mapper, authorization, credential mapper, and audit providers support identity domains. Remove any providers that do not support identity domains.

message: Security shutting down retired instance of security realm: {0}
cause: This message is for informational purposes.
action: No action is required.

message: Security pre-initializing using security realm: {0}
cause: This message is for informational purposes.
action: Verify that the correct security realm is configured.

message: Security post-initializing using security realm: {0}
cause: This message is for informational purposes.
action: Verify that the correct security realm is configured.

message: Security starting security realm: {0}
cause: This message is for informational purposes.
action: Verify that the correct security realm is being restarted.

message: Security completed starting security realm: {0}
cause: This message is for informational purposes.
action: Verify that the correct security realm is started.

message: The realm "{0}" failed during shut down: {1}.
cause: There are multiple possible causes.
action: Carefully read the returned error to determine if any action is required.

message: Failed operation using security realm: {0}
cause: There are multiple possible causes.
action: Carefully read the returned error to determine if any action is required.

message: Provider configured for identity domain(s) "{0}" but login was supplied identity domain {1}
cause: The partition or security configuration may be configured with a different identity domain than the provider.
action: None required if the realm handles multiple identity domains. Otherwise, the security configuration may have inconsistent identity domains.

message: These configuration changes cannot be activated because the Administrative Identity Domain must be set on the Security Configuration when Identity Domains are in use.
cause: The "Administrative Identity Domain" attribute is not set for the domain's SecurityConfiguration.
action: To correct the problem, set the "AdministrativeIdentityDomain" for the domain's SecurityConfiguration.

message: These configuration changes cannot be activated because the Primary Identity Domain must be set on Partition {0} when Identity Domains are in use.
cause: The "Primary Identity Domain" attribute is not set for the specified Partition.
action: To correct the problem, set the "PrimaryIdentityDomain" for the specified Partition.

message: These configuration changes cannot be activated because there is no authenticator configured to support the "{0}" Identity Domain value.
cause: No authenticator in this realm is configured to support the specified Identity Domain value.
action: To correct the problem, configure one of the authenticator providers to support the specified identity domain. This can either be a WebLogic Server provider or a custom provider.

message: Both permissions.xml and a deployment descriptor grant were found.
cause: Both permissions.xml and a deployment descriptor grant were used to declare permissions.
action: Use either permissions.xml or a deployment descriptor grant to declare permissions for the application. Use only one and remove the other before redeployment.

message: One or more of the declared permissions in permissions.xml are not allowed.
cause: The application's permissions.xml file contains one or more permissions which are not allowed.
action: Remove the prohibited permissions from the permissions.xml file before redeploying the application.

message: Security permissions declared in the "{0}" deployment descriptor are not allowed due to the server''s configuration.
cause: The server is configured to not allow Java security permissions to be declared in the Weblogic deployment descriptors.
action: Remove Java security permissions from the deployment descriptor or change the server's security configuration before redeployment.

message: Java EE packaged permissions are not allowed due to the server''s configuration.
cause: The server is configured to not allow Java EE packaged permissions in the application.
action: Remove permissions.xml or change the server's security configuration before redeployment.

message: The serverr''s SSL configuration is not available. There will potentially be SSL handshake failures.
cause: The server has not been able to load its SSL configuration. This usually occurs early in server startup, before Management Service runtime access is available.
action: Verify that the Administration Server is running. If the Administration Server non-secure port is disabled, verify that the Managed Server default SSL configuration is sufficient to handshake with the Administration Server over the SSL port.

message: These configuration changes cannot be activated because the realm "{0}" already exists.
cause: There is more than one realm named "{0}" configured for the domain's SecurityConfiguration.
action: To correct the problem, change the name of each realm configured for the domain's SecurityConfiguration to be a unique name.

message: These configuration changes cannot be activated because the realm "{0}" is not valid.
cause: The realm "{0}" is not properly configured for the domain's SecurityConfiguration. See the nested exceptions that accompany this error for details.
action: To correct the problem, review and address the nested exceptions that accompany this error.

message: The {0} provider imports a modified entry: {1}
cause: The security provider needs to be modified because of the overwrite flag is open.
action: Modified the imported data from the existing one.

message: The connection to the datasource using "{0}" could not be established due to the following error: {1}.
cause: There are multiple possible causes.
action: Read the returned exception to determine if any action is required.

message: Unable to create policy with type {0}. Got exception {1}.
cause: An internal WebLogic server error occurred when creating policy type {0}.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: A failure has occurred checking for WebLogic Kernel Permission: {0}
cause: An attempt to perform an action that requires privileges has failed.
action: Read the returned exception to determine what action is required.

message: LDAP test connection failed: {0} not configured.
cause: The connection parameters provided were incomplete.
action: Verify that the connection parameters provided are complete.

message: Error processing test connection parameters {0}: {1}
cause: The connection parameters provided were incorrect.
action: Verify that the connection parameters provided are correct.

message: Identity Assertion type cannot be null
cause: The token type was null.
action: Null should not be passed as the token type.

message: Identity Assertion token cannot be null
cause: The identity assertion token was null.
action: Null should not be passed as the token.

message: Identity Assertion type of {0} is not a valid token type for the OpenID Connect Identity Assertion provider.
cause: The token type passed in did not match the OpenID Connect Identity Assertion token type.
action: Pass in the correct token type for the OpenID Connect Identity Assertion provider.

message: Resource type already registered: "{0}"
cause: The supplied resource type has already been registered.
action: Specify a valid resource type.

message: Ldap request wait exceeds time limit {0} milliseconds, try number count {1}
cause: Ldap request takes unexpected long time.
action: None required. Otherwise check if the Ldap authentication provider configuration is optimized or Ldap server issue.

message: File does not exist {0}
cause: SerializedSystemIni.dat does not exist in the specified location.
action: Create a SerializedSystemIni.dat in the specified location if needed.

message: The connection between the WebLogic servers cannot be authorized since the nonce value has expired. Verify that clock skew has not occurred between the servers in the domain.
cause: Clock skew between the two servers may cause the client nonce to be expired when it is validated on the server.
action: Verify that the clocks on the servers in the domain are in agreement. Optionally, enable the Network Time Protocol daemon on the servers in the domain.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but no auditing provider is configured in realm: {0}.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but no auditing provider is configured.
action: None required otherwise set SecureModeMBean WarnOnAuditing to false to avoid this message.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but the Default Auditor logging level does not include WARNING, ERROR, and FAILURE audit records in realm: {0}.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but the Default Auditor logging level does not include WARNING, ERROR, and FAILURE audit records.
action: None required otherwise set SecureModeMBean WarnOnAuditing to false to avoid this message.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but user lockout settings are not secure in realm: {0}, i.e. LockoutThreshold should not be greater than 5, LockoutDuration should not be less than 30.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but user lockout is not enabled or lockout settings are less restrictive than default.
action: This is a warning message, no action is required.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but unencrypted password is used in command line, i.e. system property weblogic.management.password is set.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but unencrypted password is used in command line.
action: This is a warning message, no action is required.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but no password validator is configured in realm: {0}.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but no password validator is configured.
action: This is a warning message, no action is required.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but the Security Manager is not enabled.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but the Security Manager is not enabled.
action: None required otherwise set SecureModeMBean WarnOnJavaSecurityManager to false to avoid this message.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but PostBind is not enabled for machine : {0} when unix machine is configured and port {1} < 1024 is used.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but PostBind is not enabled when unix machine is configured and port < 1024 is used.
action: This is a warning message, no action is required.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but the the administration port is not enabled.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but the the administration port is not enabled.
action: This is a warning message, no action is required.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but the fie or directory {0} owner {1} does not match the domain directory owner {2}.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but the file or directory owner does not match the domain directory owner.
action: None required otherwise set SecureModeMBean WarnOnInsecureFileSystem to false to avoid this message.

message: Secure Mode is enabled but the the file or directory {0} is insecure since its permission is not a minimum of umask 027.
cause: Secure Mode is enabled but the the file or directory permission is insecure.
action: None required otherwise change the file or directory permission to be a minimum of umask 027, or set SecureModeMBean WarnOnInsecureFileSystem to false to avoid this message.

message: Secure Mode requires a realm with updated default authorizations policies. The realm {0} does not contain the correct default policies. Create a new realm and reapply any custom policies.
cause: Secure Mode support requires updated default authorization policies.
action: Create a new realm with the same providers as the existing realm. Transfer the custom security policies from the old realm to the new realm and set the realm attribute to point to the new realm.

message: SSLv3 is enabled by the system property {0} in secure mode. SSLv3 is vulnerable and should not be enabled.
cause: SSLv3 is enabled by the system property in secure mode.
action: Enable a secure TLS version such as TLSv1.2 or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Basic Constraints extension validation is turned off by the system property {0}=off in secure mode.
cause: Basic Constraints extension validation is turned off by the system property {0}=off in secure mode.
action: Turn on Basic Constraints extension validation, {0}=strong or {0}=true or {0}=strict, or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: SSL hostname verification is disabled by the system property {0} in secure mode.
cause: SSL hostname verification is disabled by the system property {0} in secure mode.
action: Enable SSL host name verification or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: SSL hostname verification is disabled by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
cause: SSL hostname verification is disabled by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
action: Enable SSL host name verification or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: SSL hostname verification is disabled by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
cause: SSL hostname verification is disabled by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
action: Enable SSL host name verification or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: SSLv3 is enabled as the minimum TLS protocol version by the system property {0} in secure mode.
cause: SSLv3 is enabled as the minimum TLS protocol version by the system property {0} in secure mode.
action: Configure a secure TLS version such as TLSv1.2 as the minimum TLS protocol version or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: SSLv3 is enabled as the minimum TLS protocol version by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
cause: SSLv3 is enabled as the minimum TLS protocol version by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
action: Configure a secure TLS version such as TLSv1.2 as the minimum TLS protocol version or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: SSLv3 is enabled as the minimum TLS protocol version by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
cause: SSLv3 is enabled as the minimum TLS protocol version by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
action: Configure a secure TLS version such as TLSv1.2 as the minimum TLS protocol version or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Null cipher suites are allowed by the system property {0} in secure mode.
cause: Null cipher suites are allowed by the system property {0} in secure mode.
action: Null cipher suites are not secure and should not be allowed or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Null cipher suites are allowed by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
cause: Null cipher suites are allowed by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
action: Null cipher suites are not secure and should not be allowed or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Null cipher suites are allowed by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
cause: Null cipher suites are allowed by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
action: Null cipher suites are not secure and should not be allowed or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Anonymous cipher suites are allowed by the system property {0} in secure mode.
cause: Anonymous cipher suites are allowed by the system property {0} in secure mode.
action: Anonymous cipher suites are not secure and should not be allowed or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: TLS client initiated secure renegotation is enabled by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
cause: TLS client initiated secure renegotation is enabled by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode.
action: Disable TLS client initiated secure renegotation or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: TLS client initiated secure renegotation is enabled by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
cause: TLS client initiated secure renegotation is enabled by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode.
action: Disable TLS client initiated secure renegotation or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Insecure cipher suites are configured by the SSL configuration of server {0} in secure mode: {1}
cause: Insecure cipher suites are configured for secure mode.
action: Remove the insecure cipher suites {1} or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Insecure cipher suites are configured by the SSL configuration of server channel {0} in secure mode: {1}.
cause: Insecure cipher suites are configured for secure mode.
action: Remove the insecure cipher suites {1} or turn off warning on insecure SSL configuration in secure mode.

message: Secure Mode requires that users in the Administrators group do not have obvious user names. Change the user name "{0}" so it is not a commonly used administrator name.
cause: A user that is a member of the Administrators group has an obvious user name.
action: Change the user name to non-obvious name.

message: Secure Mode requires that the samples are not installed in the WebLogic installation directory.
cause: The WebLogic installation contains the samples directory
action: Reinstall WebLogic and do not include the samples when choosing the installation type.

message: Boot authentication attempt failed. Retry attempt {0} of {1}
cause: The authentication for the boot user failed.
action: Wait until the server fails to boot and determine if the authentication failure can be corrected. If so, then retry the boot after correcting the authentication failure.

message: The WLS SSL-ALPN jars are not found in java.endorsed.dirs directory. The HTTP/2 over SSL (h2) protocol will not be supported.
cause: WLS can't find SSL-ALPN related classes because WLS SSL-ALPN related jars are not found in java.endorsed.dirs directory.
action: Check the system property java.endorsed.dirs is configured and make sure WLS SSL-ALPN jars are in the directory.

message: The Oracle Identity Cloud Service returned '{0}' 429 status code responses (Too Many Requests) in the last '{1}' seconds.
cause: The Oracle Identity Cloud Service response indicating user has sent too many reqests in a given amount of time.
action: No action is required.

message: The Oracle Identity Cloud Service returned '{0}' service unavailable or network timeout responses in the last '{1}' seconds.
cause: The Oracle Identity Cloud Service unavailable or timeout caused by network issue or other issues.
action: Verify that the network or the Oracle Identity Cloud Service is not experiencing issues.

message: Artifact of type SAML2 artifact-04 is not successfully constructed.
cause: The passing parameter {0} was null or its length was not {1}.
action: Check the parameters being passed to the SAMLArtifact constructor.

message: SAML message returned in samlp:ArtifactResponse is not the right type. It should be of {0} type.
cause: The SAML message that was returned in samlp:ArtifactResponse was not the one that samlp:ArtifactRequest requested.
action: Check the remote ARS storage to see if there is a {0} corresponding to the artifact.

message: There is no SAML message in returned samlp:ArtifactResponse.
cause: The SAML message may have been deleted because of expiration, or the client has no authority to access it.
action: Check the remote ARS storage to see if there is an item corresponding to the artifact.

message: The artifact in the HTTP request is null. It should be {0} parameter in the HTTP request.
cause: The artifact in the HTTP request was null. It should be {0} parameter in the HTTP request.
action: Check the HTTP request to see if there is a {0} parameter with a non-null value.

message: Could not get binding handler of {0} type.
cause: An internal WebLogic Server error occurred while getting the binding handler.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Could not get SAML response, it should be of {0} type.
cause: The remote service may be down or the message exchange timed out.
action: Check the remote service status.

message: The SAML response is invalid in the field {0}, expected value is {1}, actual value is {2}.
cause: The SAML response was not the one that the SAML request expected.
action: Check the remote service configuration.

message: Could not get end point, the entityID is {0}, service type is {1}, end point index is {2}.
cause: The end point was not registered.
action: Check the register storage to see if there is such an endpoint.

message: Cannot get the key to sign {0}.
cause: There may not be a key to sign {0}.
action: Check the local configuration to see if there is a key for signing {0}.

message: Cannot get the key to verify the signature of {0}.
cause: There may not be a key to verify the signature of {0}.
action: Check the partner registration information to see if there is key for verifying {0}.

message: There is no SAML message in the HTTP request, it should be a value of {0} parameter.
cause: There was no message in the HTTP request with a value of {0}.
action: Check the HTTP request to confirm that there is a parameter with a non-null value.

message: The certificate chain sent by the SSL server is null or empty.
cause: The remote partner acting as an SSL server did not send a certificate to authenticate itself.
action: Check the remote partner to see if it has an SSL certificate to authenticate itself.

message: The SSL server cannot be trusted for {0}.
cause: The remote partner acting as an SSL server cannot be trusted.
action: Check the SSL certificate that the remote partner uses to authenticate itself.

message: Could not get partner, the entityID(or SHA-1 hash value) is {0}.
cause: The partner is not registered.
action: Check the register storage to see if there is such a partner.

message: Could not get Transport Layer Certificate from partner whose entityId is {0}.
cause: The partner did not register its Transport Layer Certificate information.
action: Check the register storage to see if this partner has a Transport Layer Certificate.

message: The SSL client is expected to authenticate, but cannot get the key and certificate.
cause: The SSL key and certificate could not be retrieved.
action: Check the metadata to see if there is a Transport Layer Certificate and key.

message: Could not get SAML signature from the HTTP request, it should be a {0} parameter.
cause: If there is no SAML signature in the HTTP request, there should also be no signature algorithm in the HTTP request.
action: Check the HTTP request. The signature and signature algorithm should appear at the same time.

message: The SAML message was signed but could not get a signature from it.
cause: The SAML message was signed but there was no signature to retrieve.
action: Check the SAML message for the signature.

message: The signature is invalid.
cause: The signature was verified but was not valid.
action: Provide a valid signature.

message: Could not get artifact when storing the SAML message, have no idea whether the SAML message was stored successfully.
cause: There may have been an error when generating the artifact.
action: Check the log files for an error.

message: Could not receive a valid SAML request of type "{0}" from HTTP request.
cause: A valid SAML request of type "{0}" was not received from the HTTP request.
action: Check whether the configuration of the corresponding partner is correct.

message: Could not receive a valid SAML response of type "{0}" from HTTP request.
cause: A valid SAML response of type "{0}" was not retrieved from the HTTP request.
action: Check whether the configuration of the corresponding partner is correct.

message: Error occurs when signing {0}.
cause: Error occurs when signing {0}.
action: Error occurs when signing {0}.

message: Authentication failed.
cause: Authentication failed due to an invalid certificate or an invalid username or password.
action: Ensure that your certificate or your username and password are correct.

message: Invalid Identity Assertion token object for specified token type.
cause: An internal WebLogic server error occurred while determining the token type.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Signature required but assertion is not signed.
cause: An unsigned SAML assertion was received where the SAML configuration required a signed assertion.
action: Check the SAML partner configuration or contact the SAML Identity Provider.

message: There must be a subject in assertion.
cause: A SAML assertion was received in which the SAML Subject was not provided.
action: Contact the SAML Identity Provider for information on how to correct the problem.

message: No SubjectConfirmation in assertion.
cause: A SAML assertion was received in which the SAML Subject Confirmation was not provided.
action: Contact the SAML Identity Provider for information on how to correct the problem.

message: Unsupported assertion type: {0}.
cause: A SAML assertion was received in which the SAML Subject Confirmation is not supported.
action: Contact the SAML Identity Provider for information on how to correct the problem.

message: Signature required but no valid certificate in the specified partner configuration.
cause: A signature was required but the certificate has expired, is not yet valid or is undefined in the specified partner configuration.
action: Provide a valid certificate for the partner.

message: Cannot get the key to verify the signature.
cause: An improper key was provided.
action: Provide a proper key to verify the signature.

message: The signature is invalid.
cause: The signature was verified but was not valid.
action: Provide a valid signature.

message: Invalid version found in the {0} document: {1}.
cause: The sender sent a document that is not version 2.0.
action: Fix the SAML sender.

message: Certificate in assertion''s keyinfo does not match configured signing certificate.
cause: A SAML assertion was received where the SAML KeyInfo does not match the SAML partner entry.
action: Check the SAML partner configuration or contact the SAML Identity Provider.

message: No issuer in the assertion.
cause: A SAML assertion was received where the SAML Issuer is not supplied or the SAML Issuer is empty.
action: Contact the SAML Identity Provider for information on how to correct the problem.

message: The Issuer in {0} is invalid: {1}.
cause: The sender sent an invalid SAML document that contains an invalid Issuer URI.
action: Ask the SAML requester to fix this problem.

message: Invalid issuer format: {0}.
cause: The sender sent a document that contains an invalid issuer format attribute.
action: Ask the SAML requester to send a correct document.

message: Assertion is not yet valid ({0} condition).
cause: The received SAML assertion is not yet valid.
action: Check the SAML configuration, the system clock or contact the SAML Identity Provider.

message: Assertion has expired ({0} condition).
cause: The received SAML assertion has expired.
action: Check the SAML configuration, the system clock or contact the SAML Identity Provider.

message: AudienceRestriction condition not satisfied (no matching audience).
cause: The received SAML assertion audience restrictions where not satisfied.
action: Check the SAML partner configuration or contact the SAML Identity Provider.

message: The assertion: {0} cannot be used again.
cause: The received SAML assertion has already been consumed and cannot be used again.
action: Contact the SAML Identity Provider for information on how to correct the problem.

message: Web SSO profile must be bearer confirmation method.
cause: A SAML assertion was received where the SAML Subject Confirmation must be of Bearer type.
action: Contact the SAML Identity Provider for information on how to correct the problem.

message: No NameID in assertion.
cause: A SAML assertion was received in which the SAML Issuer is not supplied or the SAML Issuer is empty.
action: Contact the SAML Identity Provider for information on how to correct the problem.

message: The partner: {0} does not exist or is disabled.
cause: The partner: {0} does not exist or is disabled.
action: Register or enable the specified partner.

message: Cannot find identity provider partner, partnerName: {0}, request URI: {1}.
cause: Cannot find identity provider partner, partnerName: {0}, request URI: {1}.
action: Check the identity provider partner setting.

message: Cannot find single sign-on services for partner: {0}.
cause: No single sign-on services are defined for partner: {0}.
action: Define single sign-on services for partner: {0}.

message: Cannot get the single sign-on sign key to sign the {0} in the key store.
cause: Either no single sign-on sign key is defined in the local configuration or the key/keystore is invalid.
action: Correct the key configuration for SAML services.

message: The status code of SAML2 response indicates that the request failed: {0}.
cause: The SAML2 response from the identity provider partner indicates that the request failed.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to get the reason.

message: Cannot find identity provider partner for issuer URI: {0}.
cause: The identity provider partner for issuer URI: {0} is not defined.
action: Define the identity provider partner for issuer URI: {0}.

message: Cannot get a valid certificate in partner registry to verify {0} for partner {1}.
cause: The certificate has expired, is not yet valid or is undefined for the specified partner.
action: Provide a valid certificate for the partner.

message: Failed to create session for identity: {0}.
cause: This usually is caused by one of the LoginSessionListeners failing to create a session.
action: Ensure that all the listeners work properly or try to re-install.

message: {0} binding of local configuration is not enabled.
cause: The binding: {0} of the local configuration is not enabled.
action: Enable the binding: {0} for the local configuration.

message: Illegal destination: {0} of assertion response.
cause: The SAML2 response received from the identity provider partner is illegal.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to ensure that the generated response is correct.

message: Cannot find request for InResponseTo: {0} in response.
cause: The SAML2 response received from the identity provider partner is illegal.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to ensure that the generated response is correct.

message: Attribute: {0} should not be empty.
cause: The SAML2 response received from the identity provider partner is illegal.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to ensure that the generated response is correct.

message: Attribute: {0} should be omitted.
cause: The SAML2 response received from the identity provider partner is illegal.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to ensure that the generated response is correct.

message: Illegal confirmation method: {0}.
cause: The SAML2 response received from the identity provider partner is illegal.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to ensure that the generated response is correct.

message: Illegal Recipient: {0} for the subject confirmation data.
cause: The SAML2 response received from the identity provider partner is illegal.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to ensure that the generated response is correct.

message: Illegal InResponseTo: {0} for the subject confirmation.
cause: The SAML2 response received from the identity provider partner is illegal.
action: Contact the identity provider partner to ensure that the generated response is correct.

message: The Issuer element cannot have NameQualifier, SPNameQualifer and SPProviderID.
cause: The SAML requester sent a document that contains an invalid <Issuer>.
action: Ask the SAML Requester to fix this.

message: Identity provider partner: {0} is not enabled.
cause: The specified identity provider partner is not enabled.
action: Enable the identity provider partner.

message: Errors occurred when sending {0}.
cause: An internal WebLogic server error occurred when sending A SAML assertion.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Errors occurred when receiving {0}.
cause: An internal WebLogic Server error occurred when A SAML assertion was requested.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Artifact requester authentication failed.
cause: There was an invalid basic auth/certificate.
action: Check the log for more details and take appropriate action.

message: The SAML2 identity provider service is disabled.
cause: The SAML2 identity provider service is disabled in the local configuration, but a request was sent to this service.
action: Enable the identity provider in the local configuration.

message: Cannot find service provider partner with the specified URI {0}.
cause: The partner may be misconfigured in the partner registry, or this issuer is not enabled/configured in the registry.
action: Configure the partner correctly in the partner registry, or just ignore it if this issuer should not be served. It could also be errors in the service provider that issues this request.

message: The service provider partner {0} is disabled.
cause: The specified service provider partner is disabled in the partner registry.
action: Enable the partner in the partner registry.

message: Cannot authenticate user in passive mode.
cause: The AuthnRequest wants the user to be authenticated in passive mode, but the Identity Provider is not able to do this.
action: Ensure that the service provider does not send passive requests, or just ignore this error.

message: The user is accessing the login return URL of SAML2 service, however, this user is not authenticated.
cause: A user accidentally accessed this page or bookmarked it, or the login application is behaving incorrectly.
action: Fix the login application.

message: There is no <AuthnRequest> object in user''s session.
cause: The user's session may have expired. The user must do the single sign-on process from the very beginning. This could also be caused by an incorrect login application.
action: Start the single sign-on process from the beginning, or fix the login application.

message: The user is accessing the Identity Provider initiator service, but is not authenticated yet.
cause: The user accidentally accessed this page or bookmarked it, or the login application is behaving incorrectly.
action: Fix the login application or the page links to this URL.

message: The Identity provider-initiated single sign-on requires a parameter {0}, but the request does not have one.
cause: The user or the link did not append the parameter.
action: Append the parameter in the request.

message: Cannot find partner with name: {0}
cause: The partner with name {0} does not exist in the partner registry.
action: Add a partner with the indicated name, or rename a partner to this name.

message: The URL for relay state is too long.
cause: The service provider-initiated single sign-on requester sent a request URL longer than 80 bytes.
action: If the URL for the service provider is too long, do not send it.

message: Received a SAML document that is not a AuthnRequest document, but {0}.
cause: The SAML requester sent an invalid document to the identity provider single sign-on service.
action: Ask the requester to send a valid document.

message: Failed to receive {0} document from the requester.
cause: Review other messages in the log for the cause.
action: Fix the error in the document or network.

message: Cannot generate assertion for the user.
cause: There may be an error in the credential mapper, or the SAML2 credential mapper is not configured correctly.
action: Configure the SAML2 credential mapper correctly.

message: Failed to unmarshal the assertion from the credential mapper.
cause: An internal WebLogic Server error occurred during unmarshaling.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: No assertion consumer services configured in the {0} partner.
cause: No assertion consumer services are configured in the partner.
action: Configure the ACS service for this partner.

message: The destination attribute of <AuthnRequest> does not match local configuration.
cause: Either the request was sent to the wrong place, or the request document is wrong.
action: Fix the SAML requester to match the destination attribute.

message: <SubjectConfirmation> cannot exist in the <Subject> element of <AuthnRequest>
cause: The SAML requester sent an <AuthnRequest>, its <subject> contains a <SubjectConfirmation>, which is not allowed by the SAML specification.
action: Fix the SAML requester.

message: Requested NameId format not supported, only urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified is supported.
cause: The SAML requester sent a request that is not supported yet.
action: Fix the SAML requester to send a request without the name id format, or set the format to "unspecified".

message: <RequestedAuthnContext> is not currently supported.
cause: The SAML requester sent an invalid <AuthnRequest> document.
action: Fix the SAML requester to send a request without <RequestedAuthnContext>.

message: The SPNameQualifier is not supported in <AuthnRequest>.
cause: The SAML requester sent a request that is not supported yet.
action: Fix the SAML requester to not send such a document.

message: Find service provider partner by issuer URI {0} error.
cause: The exception was caused while searching for the service provider partner from the store by the issuer uri.
action: Check the store service.

message: Find service provider partner by name {0} error.
cause: This exception was caused while searching for the service provider partner from the store by name.
action: Check the store service.

message: Find identity provider partner by name {0} error.
cause: This exception was caused while searching for the identity provider partner from the store by name.
action: Check the store service.

message: Find partner by name {0} error.
cause: This exception was caused while searching for the partner from the store by name.
action: Check the store service.

message: Could not get local entity ID for checking audience in assertion.
cause: The service provider has no entity ID.
action: Check the service provider local configuration to see whether it has an entity ID.

message: Could not get partner from partner registry by name.
cause: There is no such partner in the partner registry.
action: Check the partner registry to see whether this partner exists.

message: Partner is not enabled in partner registry.
cause: This partner is in the partner registry, but the partner is not enabled.
action: Check the partner registry to see whether the partner is enabled.

message: Audience cannot be empty.
cause: The assertion contains an empty audience.
action: Check the CredentialMapper.

message: Web single sign-on service provider partner entity ID is empty, could not create assertion.
cause: The SAML partner entry does not have an Entity ID or the Entity ID is empty.
action: Check and import the SAML service provider metadata file or contact the SAML service provider to obtain the Entity ID.

message: Confirmation method should not be empty.
cause: The provider partner has no confirmation method.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has a confirmation method.

message: Assertion signing certificate should not be empty while wantAssertionSigned is TRUE.
cause: The provider partner has no assertion signing certificate while wantAssertionSigned is TRUE.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has an assertion signing certificate.

message: Artifact POST form should not be empty while isArtifactUsePost is TRUE.
cause: The provider partner has no artifact POST form while isArtifactUsePost is TRUE.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has an artifact POST form.

message: Single sign-on signing certificate should not be empty while assertion/artifact request/authn request wants to be signed.
cause: The provider partner has no single sign-on signing certificate while the assertion/artifact request/authn request wants to be signed.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has a single sign-on signing certificate.

message: Issuer URI should not be empty.
cause: The provider partner has no issuer URI.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has an issuer URI.

message: No SAML2 service entity ID configured.
cause: The SAML2 service entity ID is not configured.
action: Configure the SAML2 service entity ID.

message: No SAML2 service published site URL configured.
cause: The SAML2 service published site URL is not configured.
action: Configure the SAML2 service published site URL.

message: No SAML2 service single sign-on signing key alias configured.
cause: The SAML2 service single sign-on signing key alias is not configured.
action: Configure a SAML2 service single sign-on signing key alias.

message: No SAML2 service single sign-on signing key pass phrase configured.
cause: The SAML2 service single sign-on signing key pass phrase is not configured.
action: Configure the SAML2 service single sign-on signing key pass phrase.

message: No SAML2 service login return query parameter are configured.
cause: The SAML2 service login return query parameter is not configured.
action: Configure the SAML2 service login return query parameter.

message: Entity ID should not be empty.
cause: The provider partner has no entity ID.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has an entity ID.

message: Name should not be empty.
cause: The provider partner has no name.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has a name.

message: Single sign-on services should not be empty.
cause: Provider partner has no single sign-on services.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has single sign-on services.

message: Assertion consumer services should not be empty.
cause: The provider partner has no assertion consumer services.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has assertion consumer services.

message: Illegal published site url: {0}.
cause: The published site url is illegal.
action: Check the partner information to make sure it has a legal published site url.

message: Cannot resolve IssuerURI for SAML Assertion.
cause: The system could not resolve the IssuerURI for the SAML Assertion from the context and credential mapper MBean.
action: Check the context and credential mapper MBean to make sure that the issuerURI is not empty.

message: Cannot get key info for key {0} from key service.
cause: The key info got from key service for key {0} is empty.
action: Check the key store service for key {0}.

message: Decrypt password {0} error.
cause: The password {0} could not be decrypted, causing an exception.
action: Check the key and the password.

message: Clearing cache {0}.
cause: The SAML cache is being cleared.
action: No action is required.

message: Cache {0} cleared.
cause: The SAML cache has been cleared.
action: No action is required.

message: The SAML credential cache item''s ttl value {0} is smaller than the minimum viable ttl {1} in milliseconds and will not be cached.
cause: The SAML credential cache is unable to retain the item due to the length of the timeout.
action: No action is required.

message: The value of max to return is negative.
cause: The value of max to return cannot be negative.
action: Check the value of max to return and make sure that the value is not negative.

message: The value for contact person type is illegal.
cause: The value for the contact person type is not legal. The legal value must be in one of "technical", "support", "administrative", "billing" or "other".
action: Choose one of the legal values and try again.

message: Invalid keystore configuration.
cause: The keystore configuration is not correct.
action: Configure a valid value and try again.

message: Key name is null or empty.
cause: The key name is null or empty when updating/retrieving key information.
action: Choose a valid key name and try again.

message: Invalid key information.
cause: At least one of key name, alias or passphrase is not correct.
action: Choose a valid value and try again.

message: SAML2 Service cannot be initialized:{0}
cause: Errors occurred while initializing the SAML2 service.
action: Check and change the SAML2 configuration and try again.

message: ForceAuthn and IsPassive cannot both be true.
cause: ForceAuthn wants to authenticate the user freshly, but IsPassive requests that the identity provider not do this.
action: Change one of the values to false.

message: Item has already expired on {0}, it will not be cached.
cause: The configured timeout may be too short.
action: Check the timeout configuration.

message: The "{1}" parameter passed to "{0}" must not be null.
cause: A null "{1}" parameter was passed to "{0}".
action: Ensure that a valid reference is passed.

message: The parameter MaxCacheSize or timeout must not be a negative number.
cause: The parameter MaxCacheSize or timeout cannot be a negative number.
action: Change the MaxCacheSize parameter or timeout to zero or a positive number.

message: The partner name cannot be changed at partner update operation.
cause: The user tried to change the read-only partner name.
action: Set the partner name back to the original name.

message: There is no login URL configured in Identity Provider Site.
cause: No login URL was configured in the Identity Provider Site.
action: Configure a valid login URL in SingleSignOnServicesMBean.

message: The value configured for NameMapper attribute: {0} is not valid.
cause: The name mapper class {0} is not in the classpath or it does not implement a name mapper interface.
action: Ensure that the name mapper class {0} is in the classpath and implements a name mapper interface.

message: The confirmation method passed through Context handler: {0} is not valid.
cause: An invalid confirmation method was passed through the Context handler.
action: Make sure the confirmation method passed through Context handler {0} has a valid value.

message: Using expired certificate at alias {0} for signing.
cause: An attempt was made to use an expired certificate at alias {0} for signing, but allow expired cert is false.
action: Use a valid certificate for signing.

message: Using not yet valid certificate at alias {0} for signing.
cause: A not yet valid certificate was used at alias {0} for signing, but allow expired cert is false.
action: Use a valid certificate for signing.

message: Binding type {0} is unsupported, endpoint with this binding is filtered out from partner.
cause: The binding type that was used is not supported.
action: Use a binding type that is supported.

message: The partner metadata file does not contain an XML root element of <EntityDescriptor>.
cause: The partner metadata file content is not supported.
action: Update the partner metadata content or obtain a new partner metadata file.

message: Cannot get the single sign-on sign certificate to sign the {0} in the key store.
cause: Either no single sign-on sign certificate is defined in the local configuration or the key/keystore is invalid.
action: Correct the key configuration for SAML services.

message: Error occurs when encrypting {0}.
cause: Error occurs when encrypting {0}.
action: Check debug log for specific encryption error on {0}.

message: Service info was not found for service to shut it down, {0}
cause: Service info was not found when shutting down a service.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Configuration problems detected.
cause: A bad configuration was supplied.
action: Check and fix the configuration.

message: Configuration canno be modified after the engine has been started.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine configuration was modified after the engine was started.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Engine has already been started.
cause: An attempt was made to start the engine after it had been started.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Unable to find ServiceConfig.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine cannot be configured.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Duplicate service name {0}.
cause: The service engine was configured with a duplicate service name.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Logger not found.
cause: The service engine could not find the logger.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: An attempt was made to access the ServiceEngine after it had already been shut down.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine was accessed after being shut down.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Engine already shut down.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine has already been shut down.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Service {0} not found.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine cannot find the service.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Service {0} not exposed.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine looked up a service that it cannot expose.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Service {0} does not implement ServiceLifecycleSpi.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine was configured with a service that it cannot use.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Null service {0} loaded.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine was configured with a null service.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Classloader not found for service {0}.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine was configured with a service but the service's classloader could not be found.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Failure loading service {0} using class {1}.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine could not load a service.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Service {0} is dependent upon {1} which does not exist.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine could not find a dependent service.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Service name not specified.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine could not find a service.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Attempt to remove lifecycle impl for {0} when {1} dependencies still exist.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine attempted to remove a service that still has dependencies.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Attempt to remove dependencies for {0} when no dependencies still exist.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error indicating that the service engine attempted to remove dependencies for a service when no dependencies exist.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Internal consistency failure in {0}
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Manageable Service {0} not found.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error. An attempt was made to look up a manageable service but it was not found.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: Service {0} is not manageable.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server Error. An attempt was made to look up a manageable service and a service with the requested name was found, but it was not a manageable service.
action: Contact My Oracle Support and provide them with the log files.

message: {0} requires a {1} to be supplied for configuration but it was not supplied.
cause: The configuration supplied to {0} was incorrect.
action: Ensure that the configuration supplied for {0} is correct.

message: {1} supplied to {0} is missing {2}
cause: The configuration supplied to {0} was incorrect.
action: Ensure that the configuration supplied for {0} is correct.

message: MaxIdentitiesInCache value for the IdentityCacheService is invalid {0}
cause: The value supplied for MaxIdentitiesInCache for the IdentityCacheService was invalid.
action: Change MaxIdentitiesInCache to a valid value.

message: The configured CertPathBuilder service returned a non X.509 certificate path.
cause: The CertPathBuilder was configured with an incorrect certificate path for an X.509 certificate.
action: Correct the configured CertPathBuilder service.

message: AdjudicationService does not have an AdjudicatorV2Name but has multiple AccessDecision providers.
cause: The AdjudicationService has multiple AccessDecision providers, but there are no providers that implement the AdjudicatorV2 interface.
action: Configure an adjudicator that implements the AdjudicatorV2 interface.

message: None of the configured {0} support JAAS login.
cause: There are no authentication providers configured to support JAAS login.
action: Configure at least one authentication provider that supports JAAS login.

message: An AuthorizationProvider returned a null AccessDecision.
cause: An AuthorizatonProvider was not able to return an AccessDecision.
action: Examine the AuthorizationProvider.

message: {0} may only be called if hasChallengeIdentityCompleted returns false.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error.
action: Contact My Oracle Support.

message: {0} may only be called if hasChallengeIdentityCompleted returns true.
cause: This is an internal WebLogic Server error.
action: Contact My Oracle Support.

message: {0} returned a null {1}.
cause: There is a configuration problem because a null object was returned.
action: Examine the specific objects referenced in the error message to see what may be the cause.

message: Got back a null {0} object.
cause: There is a configuration problem because a null object was returned.
action: Examine the specific object referenced in the error message to see what may be the cause.

message: Expected a {0} object but did not get one.
cause: There is a configuration problem because an object was expected and a different object was returned.
action: Check the configuration.

message: Multiple challenges not supported.
cause: An attempt has made to handle multiple challenges with a provider that does not support it.
action: Correct the code or configure a ChallengeIdentityAsserter that supports multiple challenges.

message: Incorrect Cert Path type, expecting a {0} type.
cause: The wrong type of Cert Path was passed due to incorrect configuration.
action: Correct the configured CertPathBuilder service.

message: Multiple {0} support the token type {1}.
cause: The configuration is not correct.
action: Remove one of the objects supporting this token token type from the configuration.

message: Unsupported token type {0}
cause: No objects are configured to support the specified token type.
action: Configure an object that supports this token type.

message: Unable to validate a non X.509 certificate path.
cause: Not all certificate paths are of type X.509.
action: Correct the configured CertPathBuilder service to ensure that all certificate paths are of type X.509.

message: A certificate is self signed but it is not the last certificate in the chain, see {0}
cause: There is a certificate chain but one of the certificates is self signed.
action: Ensure that the certificate chain is correct.

message: Issue DN mismatch error. Issued: {0} does not match issuer: {1}.
cause: There is an inconsistent certificate chain. The issuer DN of a certificate did not match the subject DN of the next certificate in the chain.
action: Ensure that the the certificates in the chain correctly refer to each other.

message: Certificate not signed by issuer. Certificate: {0} not signed by issuer: {1}.
cause: There is an inconsistent certificate chain. A certificate was not signed by the next certificate in the chain.
action: Ensure that the the certificates in the chain correctly refer to each other.

message: Capacity {0} greater than maximum {1}
cause: The specified queue capacity is too large.
action: Reduce the specified queue capacity.

message: Maximum capacity {0} is greater than the allowed {1}
cause: The specified maximum queue capacity is larger than allowed.
action: Reduce the specified maximum capacity.

message: Initial specified capacity of {0} is illegal.
cause: The specified capacity is illegal.
action: Specify a correct capacity.

message: Illegal load factor.
cause: An illegal load factor was specified.
action: Specify a load factor greater than 0 and less than 1.

message: No appropriate registered configuration to handle the AppConfigurationEntry named: {0}
cause: No appropriate JAASConfiguration has been registered.
action: Register the appropriate JAASConfiguration

message: Null JAASConfiguration.
cause: An attempt was made to register a null JAASConfiguration.
action: Register a non-null JAASConfiguration.

message: Duplicate JAASConfiguration.
cause: An attempt was made to register a JAASConfiguration twice.
action: Do not attempt to register the JAASConfiguration twice.

message: JAASConfiguration not registered.
cause: An attempt was made to unregister a JAASConfiguration that is not registered.
action: Do not attempt to unregister an unregistered JAASConfiguration.

message: A null {0} was specified.
cause: This method cannot accept a null argument.
action: Do not pass in a null argument.

message: The principal validators {0} and {1} both validate the same principal types.
cause: There can only be one principal validator for each principal type.
action: Correct the configuration.

message: No {1} found in method {0}
cause: Objects should have been configured but were not.
action: Correct the configuration.

message: Method {0} was unable to sign a principal.
cause: Something was wrong with the principal or the principal validator was incorrect.
action: Correct the configuration.

message: Null parameter supplied to method {0}.
cause: A parameter was not correctly specified. This is most likely a coding error.
action: Check the code for an incorrectly specified parameter.

message: SecurityProvider service class name for {0} is not specified.
cause: The SecurityProviderConfig does not contain a SecurityProvider service class name.
action: Examine the specific objects referenced in the error message to see what may be the cause, and take appropriate action.

message: Failed to obtain an instance of class {0}.
cause: There is a possible configuration problem. A {0} class name is invalid, or is not in the class path. It does not have a public no argument constructor, or has the constructor which failed to execute.
action: Check the configuration.

message: The configuration does not define a store, but providers that require a store are in use.
cause: There is no store servoce defined in the configuration.
action: Configure a store service.

message: The {0} service can not be found for {1}.

cause: An initiator with String type is not supported by NegotaiteCredentialMapper for method getCredential().
action: Try the method getCredentials() with Subject as the initiator type.

message: The MaxPasswordLength must not be less than MinAlphaCharacters+MinNumericOrSpecialCharacters.
cause: The MaxPasswordLength is less than MinAlphaCharacters+MinNumericOrSpecialCharacters.
action: Change the value and try again.

message: The MaxPasswordLength must not be less than MinLowercaseCharacters+MinUppercaseCharacters+MinNumericOrSpecialCharacters.
cause: The MaxPasswordLength is less than MinLowercaseCharacters+MinUppercaseCharacters+MinNumericOrSpecialCharacters.
action: Change the value and try again.

message: The number of numeric or special characters in a password cannot be less than {0}.
cause: The number of numeric or special characters in the password is less than {0}.
action: Add more numeric or special characters into your password.

message: The LDAP authentication provider named "{0}" failed to make a connection to LDAP server at {1}://{2}:{3}, the error cause is: {4}.
cause: Exception in making connection to LDAP server by the LDAP authentication provider.
action: Check the configured host name and port of the LDAP server for the failed LDAP authentication provider; also check SSL configuration details if SSL is enabled. If the failed provider is not used anymore in the domain, remove it to avoid the warning message. To change the default log interval (600 seconds), use the system property "weblogic.security.providers.authentication.connWarningLogIntervalSeconds". Note: If multiple LDAP providers are configured, the failover mechanism may tolerate failures for some providers.

message: The special character "{0}" cannot be the first character of a password.
cause: The special character "{0}" cannot be the first character of a password.
action: Change the value and try again.

message: Invalid property value: {0}={1}
cause: An exception occurred while processing the value for the specified property.
action: Correct the property and restart. This exception should not normally occur as the properties are only used internally.

message: Caught exception while evaluating policy expression: {0} associated with: {1}
cause: The evaluate method of the predicate used by the policy failed with a RuntimeException.
action: Ensure that parameters which are passed to the predicates in the expression or context are valid.

message: An invalid predicate {0} was retrieved from the database. Ensure that the predicate class is in the server classpath.
cause: The class file belonging to the condition was not in the server classpath.
action: Update the classpath and restart the server.

message: Evaluation of policy associated with role {0} failed because the policy expression contains unregistered predicate: {1}.
cause: The predicate that is specified in the log message is not registered, or the policy is out of date.
action: Register the predicate or remove its reference from the policy.

message: Evaluation of policy associated with resource {0} failed because the policy expression contains unregistered predicate: {1}.
cause: The predicate that is specified in the log message is not registered, or the policy is out of date.
action: Register the predicate, or remove its reference from the policy.

message: Invalid call to the deprecated method {0}.
cause: The method was deprecated in the latest servlet specification.
action: Check the specification for an alternative. If not corrected, unpredictable behavior might occur.

message: Could not remove session: "{0}".
cause: An unexpected error occurred while removing the session.
action: Check the exception text for the exact error message.

message: HTTP session file persistence: storing sessions in directory: {0}.
cause: This is an informational message.
action: Ensure that the directory stores the sessions per the PeristentStoreDir session parameter (weblogic.xml).

message: Erasing the directory tree for persistent HTTP sessions: {0}.
cause: The storage directory is being deleted as a result of session invalidation.
action: Ensure that deletion is occurring while removing sessions.

message: Cannot delete "{0}".
cause: The storage directory failed to be deleted.
action: Ensure that the permissions on this directory are correct and that the file and directory are not in use by another process.

message: An error occurred while serializing session data. Deleting this session.
cause: This might be due to a change in the class definition or serialVersionUID.
action: Check the exception text for the exact error message and take appropriate action.

message: {0} not invalidated. The session that was serialized into the file system does not exist.
cause: The session was not found and does not exist.
action: This session might have been deleted by a trigger in another server, which is pointing to the same storage location.

message: Deleted file-based session persistent store file: {0}.
cause: This is an informational message indicating that the storage file is being deleted.
action: Ensure that the deletion is consistent with the path defined in the PersistentStoreDir (weblogic.xml)

message: Session expiration check failed for: {0}. Invalidating the session expiration check.
cause: An unexpected error occurred while checking the session for expiration.
action: The session will be removed from the persistent store. Check the exception text for the exact error message.

message: An error occurred while loading the serialized session data for session {0}.
cause: An unexpected error occurred.
action: Check the exception text for the exact error message. The file could be corrupted.

message: The file-based persistent session ID: "{0}" was stored in file: "{1}". File exists: {2}.
cause: This is an informational message indicating that the session was stored in this path.
action: Ensure that the file-based persistent session storage is consistent with the PersistentStoreDir (weblogic.xml).

message: Received malformed cookie ID: {0}.
cause: The cookie could not be parsed properly.
action: Check the cookie format and the exception text for the exact problem.

message: Session invalidation interval: {0}
cause: The session invalidation trigger is being created.
action: Ensure that the session invalidation interval is consistent with the InvalidationIntervalSecs (weblogic.xml).

message: An unexpected error occurred in the HTTP session timeout callback while deleting sessions.
cause: The invalidation trigger failed unexpectedly while deleting sessions.
action: Check the exception message for the probable cause.

message: An unexpected error occurred in the HTTP session timeout callback while checking session expiration.
cause: An unexpected error occurred while checking the sessions for expiration.
action: Check the exception text for the exact error message.

message: The session data could not be deserialized.
cause: The session data could not be deserialized.
action: Ensure that the object being put into the session is serializable and that the non-transient objects it aggregates are also serializable. If any of the non-transient objects in the entire object graph are not serializable, this error message will occur.

message: HTTP session expired. Session ID: {0}
cause: This is an informational message indicating that the session with ID {0} has expired.
action: If the session expired sooner than expected, check the SessionTimeout parameter in the weblogic.xml file.

message: An error occurred while unregistering the servlet session runtime MBean: {0}.
cause: This MBean could not be removed.
action: Check the exception text for the exact error message.

message: An error occurred while creating a servlet session runtime MBean.
cause: This MBean could not be created.
action: Check the exception text for the exact error message.

message: The license for replicated sessions is invalid: {0}. Downgrading sessions to memory, which are not replicated.
cause: The license was not valid to support replicated sessions.
action: Check the license. An upgrade to a clustering and replication license may be desired. Alternatively, file and JDBC persistence can be used for session store reliability.

message: The timer invalidated session ID: {0}, Web application: {1}, because it expired.
cause: This message was logged by the Web application level timer, which periodically checks the sessions for expiration. The session expired.
action: If the session expired too soon, check the SessionTimeout in the weblogic.xml file.

message: Unknown persistent type: {0} for Web application: {1}. Valid types are: memory, file, replicated, JDBC, cookie, and replicated_if_clustered.
cause: The specified persistent store type for this Web application was not recognized.
action: Check the PersistentStoreType parameter in the weblogic.xml file. The valid values are: memory, file, replicated, cookie, JDBC, and replicated_if_clustered.

message: Replicated HTTP sessions were specified for the Web application: {0}, but clustering was not enabled.
cause: The session replication mechanism relies on clustering, which was not been configured.
action: Set up clustering or change the PersistentStoreType in the weblogic.xml file to either memory, file, JDBC, or cookie.

message: The getAttribute(): {0} was a handle. Reconstructing the EJB object.
cause: This is an informational message indicating that the container is storing only the handle for the EJB object placed into the session.
action: No action is required.

message: The setAttribute(): {0} was an EJB object. Storing the handle into the session.
cause: The container found an EJB handle stored in the session. It will reconstruct the EJB object before returning it.
action: No action is required.

message: An error occurred while reconstructing the EJB object: {0}, which was put into the session.
cause: The container found an EJB handle stored in the session. However, it failed to reconstruct the EJB object using that handle.
action: Ensure that the handle is still valid. Check the exception text for the exact error message.

message: An error occurred while finding the handle for the EJB object: {0}.
cause: The container stores only the handle for an EJB object put into the session. However, in this case, it failed to find the handle.
action: Check the RemoteException for the exact error message details.

message: Found WLCookie cookie {0}={1}.
cause: The cookie persistence mechanism relies on setting the cookie header.
